sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 0.3g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a fruit, sugar moderation is the whole game.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 7.7g. sugar, fibre, and glycemic index are doing most of the work. for fruit, the tradeoff is sweetness versus fibre, satiety, and overall payoff.

sections.narration: vitamin c at 65% DV, vitamin a at 0% DV stand out most. that helps, but vitamins still are not the main selling point.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: potassium at 3% DV, magnesium at 3% DV, iron at 2% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.
sections.subtitleText: potassium at 3% DV, magnesium at 3% DV, iron at 2% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first. sweetness is kept unusually well in check because that makes the fruit profile much cleaner than average. easy to use as a whole food because simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. brings a clean fruit identity because it still contributes something valuable even when it is not elite.

sections.subtitleText: the upsides first. sweetness is kept unusually well in check because that makes the fruit profile much cleaner than average. easy to use as a whole food because simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. brings a clean fruit identity because it still contributes something valuable even when it is not elite.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next. protein contribution is tiny because that is normal for fruit, but it still limits nutritional breadth. sugar can climb fast because a lot of fruits look healthy but still lean sweet. satiety is not especially strong because fruit often needs help from the rest of the meal.
